Best Demand Forecasting Tools for Smarter Inventory Planning

Accurate demand forecasting is no longer optional—it’s essential for businesses competing in today’s fast-moving, multichannel environment.

Choosing the right demand forecasting tool can mean the difference between stockouts and excess inventory. In this guide, we compare the best demand forecasting tools for e-commerce brands, mid-market retailers, and enterprise businesses in 2026.

In this article, we explore the top demand forecasting tools available today—from agile SaaS platforms like Verve AI to enterprise ERP solutions.

1. Verve AI (Best for Shopify & WooCommerce Brands)

Verve AI is a Shopify-focused SaaS app that makes advanced demand forecasting accessible for direct-to-consumer brands.

Key Features:

  • AI-driven forecasts based on historical sales.

  • Variant-level forecasting for total accuracy

  • Safety stock and reorder recommendations.

  • Direct Shopify integration with setup in minutes.

Pros: Simple, e-commerce-first, fast ROI.
Cons: Newer compared to legacy systems.
Best For: Shopify and D2C merchants looking for scalable forecasting.

2. Inventory Planner

A widely used SaaS app for Shopify, Amazon, and BigCommerce.

Key Features:

  • Demand forecasting and replenishment planning.

  • Purchase order automation.

  • Multichannel support.

Pros: Strong ecommerce focus, trusted by many brands.
Cons: Pricing complexity, learning curve.
Best For: Growing e-commerce businesses.

3. Prediko

Prediko is another Shopify-first demand forecasting tool designed for scaling brands.

Key Features:

  • Automated forecasting and replenishment.

  • Purchase order generation.

  • Supplier planning tools.

Pros: Intuitive UI, easy adoption.
Cons: Limited outside Shopify ecosystem.
Best For: Shopify brands scaling their product catalogs.

4. NetSuite Demand Planning

An ERP module offering advanced demand planning features.

Key Features:

  • Forecasting across finance, operations, and supply chain.

  • Scenario planning and replenishment.

  • Integration with NetSuite ERP.

Pros: Enterprise-grade, robust.
Cons: Expensive, requires dedicated teams.
Best For: Mid-to-large enterprises.

More time, More Sales

AI Forecasting For E-Commerce Merchants

ForShopify

For WooCommerce

5. SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP)

SAP IBP provides global corporations with demand forecasting and planning capabilities.

Key Features:

  • Demand sensing powered by AI.

  • Scenario modeling and supply planning.

  • Integration with SAP ERP systems.

Pros: Extremely powerful for global supply chains.
Cons: High cost, steep implementation requirements.
Best For: Multinational enterprises.

6. Oracle Demand Management Cloud

Oracle’s cloud-based solution focuses on enterprise-scale demand management.

Key Features:

  • Predictive analytics for demand sensing.

  • Integrated with Oracle ERP Cloud.

  • AI and machine learning models.

Pros: Highly scalable, reliable.
Cons: Cost and complexity limit accessibility.
Best For: Large companies with complex supply chains.

7. o9 Solutions

An AI-powered integrated planning platform.

Key Features:

  • Demand forecasting and sensing.

  • Scenario planning and “what if” analysis.

  • End-to-end business planning.

Pros: Cutting-edge AI, flexible modeling.
Cons: Enterprise-focused, higher cost.
Best For: Complex supply chain environments.

8. Anaplan

Anaplan specializes in connected planning across finance, sales, and operations.

Key Features:

  • Demand forecasting with collaborative features.

  • Advanced scenario planning.

  • Cloud-based and highly configurable.

Pros: Strong for financial alignment.
Cons: Requires expertise to implement effectively.
Best For: Enterprises needing integrated planning.

9. RELEX Solutions

A retail-focused demand forecasting platform.

Key Features:

  • Forecasting and replenishment for stores.

  • Promotion and markdown optimization.

  • Strong analytics capabilities.

Pros: Designed for retail operations.
Cons: Large retailer pricing, niche focus.
Best For: Large retailers with physical store networks.

10. Kinaxis RapidResponse

A supply chain planning platform with strong demand forecasting modules.

Key Features:

  • Real-time scenario analysis.

  • Demand planning and S&OP (Sales & Operations Planning).

  • Strong for fast-moving supply chains.

Pros: Agile, dynamic for manufacturers.
Cons: Enterprise-level learning curve.
Best For: Global manufacturers and brands.

Which software is for me?

Conclusion

From lightweight SaaS tools like Verve AI to enterprise ERP platforms like SAP and Oracle, there’s a demand forecasting tool for every type of business.

  • E-commerce brands benefit from tools like Verve AI, Inventory Planner, and Prediko.

  • Large enterprises lean on ERP solutions like SAP, Oracle, and NetSuite.

  • Retail and manufacturing companies rely on specialized platforms like RELEX and Kinaxis.

👉 Explore more in our related guides:

  • Inventory Forecasting Tools & Technology

  • Supply Chain EDI

  • Demand Forecasting Software

  • Multichannel Inventory Control Software

Or start with Verve AI to connect your Shopify store and forecast demand with confidence.

images.alt: Verve AI - AI Forecasting for Shopify - AI Forecasting, quick and easy, no spreadsheets required | Product Hunt

htmlContent:
The Best Demand Forecasting Tools for 2026: Quick Answer

If you manage inventory for a Shopify or WooCommerce store, you already know the pain: stockouts cost you sales, overstock ties up cash, and spreadsheets stop scaling the moment you cross 50 SKUs. The right demand forecasting tool fixes all three.

Here are the best demand forecasting tools for 2026 based on our testing and experience:

  1. Verve AI — Best for Shopify & WooCommerce merchants
  2. Prediko — Best Shopify-native forecasting app
  3. Forecastly — Best for high-volume Shopify stores
  4. Assisty — Best free/budget option for Shopify
  5. Excel + templates — Best for merchants just starting out
  6. Inventory Planner — Best for multi-channel merchants
  7. Brightpearl — Best for omnichannel retail operations
  8. Netstock — Best for mid-market distribution businesses

This guide covers pricing, key features, Shopify and WooCommerce compatibility, and who each tool is actually best suited for. We have a unique perspective here: Verve AI is one of the tools on this list, which means we can give you the honest inside view rather than a recycled listicle.

Quick Comparison: Demand Forecasting Tools at a Glance

How We Evaluated These Demand Forecasting Tools

We assessed each tool against the criteria that matter most to ecommerce merchants — not enterprise procurement teams:

  • Forecast accuracy: Does it use sales history, seasonality, and trends — or just a simple moving average?
  • Shopify and WooCommerce integration quality: Does it sync automatically, or require manual CSV exports?
  • SKU-level and variant-level forecasting: Can it forecast at the size/colour/variant level, not just product level?
  • Ease of setup: Time from install to first useful forecast (hours vs weeks)
  • Pricing transparency: Are prices published, or is everything "contact us"?
  • Support quality: Response time and helpfulness for SMB merchants

We have direct experience running Verve AI and have evaluated the others based on public documentation, Shopify App Store reviews, and feedback from customers who switched from competing tools.

The 5 Best Demand Forecasting Tools for Shopify & Ecommerce

1. Verve AI — Best for Shopify & WooCommerce Merchants

Verve AI is an AI-powered demand forecasting and inventory planning app built specifically for Shopify and WooCommerce merchants. It connects directly to your store, learns your sales patterns, and generates purchase order recommendations — without spreadsheets or manual data entry.

Key features:

  • AI demand forecasting at the SKU and variant level
  • Automated purchase order recommendations with supplier lead times factored in
  • Safety stock and reorder point calculations
  • Out-of-stock prevention alerts and replenishment triggers
  • Native Shopify and WooCommerce integration — no CSV exports required
  • Works alongside your existing demand forecasting Excel templates if you want a hybrid approach during transition

Pricing: From $24.99/month. Free trial available. No credit card required to get started.

Pros:

  • Purpose-built for ecommerce — not retrofitted from enterprise supply chain software
  • Works with both Shopify and WooCommerce natively (the only AI forecasting tool on this list that does both)
  • Setup takes under 30 minutes — no developer or data team needed
  • Variant-level forecasting handles complex catalogues (colour, size, material, bundle)
  • Lowest starting price of any AI forecasting tool on this list

Cons:

  • Not suited for enterprise ERP environments or multi-warehouse distribution operations
  • Forecast accuracy improves significantly with 90+ days of sales history — new stores see less benefit initially

Best for: Shopify or WooCommerce merchants doing $200K–$10M in annual revenue who are outgrowing spreadsheets and want AI-powered forecasting without a complex implementation project.

Rating: 5/5

2. Prediko — Best Shopify-Native Forecasting App

Prediko is a demand forecasting and inventory management app built exclusively for Shopify. It focuses on purchase order management and integrates tightly with Shopify's native product and variant structure.

Key features:

  • Demand forecasting based on sales velocity and seasonality patterns
  • Purchase order creation and management directly inside Shopify admin
  • Stockout prediction and low-stock alerts by location
  • Supplier management with lead time tracking
  • Forecasting across Shopify Online Store and POS

Pricing: Paid plans from approximately $59/month. Free plan available with limited SKUs. Check the Shopify App Store for current pricing as it changes.

Pros:

  • Deep Shopify integration — feels like a native part of the admin
  • Strong purchase order workflow built in
  • Good for merchants selling across Shopify POS and Online simultaneously
  • Clean, intuitive interface

Cons:

  • Shopify-only — no WooCommerce support
  • Forecasting model handles simple seasonality but can struggle with erratic demand patterns
  • Pricing scales with SKU count — can get expensive for large catalogues

Best for: Shopify-only merchants who want tight purchase order management built into their existing admin workflow.

Rating: 4/5

3. Forecastly — Best for High-Volume Shopify Stores

Forecastly is a demand forecasting tool designed for Shopify merchants with large catalogues and high order volumes. It focuses on statistical forecasting models and provides detailed replenishment reporting and inventory health dashboards.

Key features:

  • Statistical demand forecasting incorporating trend, seasonality, and promotional uplifts
  • Purchase order recommendations with configurable supplier lead times
  • Replenishment reports and inventory health dashboards
  • Dead stock and slow-moving inventory identification
  • API-based Shopify integration

Pricing: From approximately $49/month, scaling with order volume. Check their website for current pricing tiers as they vary by store size.

Pros:

  • Strong statistical forecasting for merchants with complex seasonal demand
  • Detailed reporting and inventory health visibility
  • Handles large SKU catalogues (1,000+ products) effectively

Cons:

  • Shopify-only — no WooCommerce or other platform support
  • Interface has a steeper learning curve compared to Verve AI or Prediko
  • Setup and forecast tuning typically takes several days to get right

Best for: High-volume Shopify stores (500+ orders/month) with complex seasonal patterns who need detailed replenishment analytics and can invest time in setup.

Rating: 4/5

4. Assisty — Best Free / Budget Option

Assisty is a Shopify analytics and inventory management app with a free tier, making it accessible for merchants who are not yet ready to invest in a paid forecasting tool. It covers basic inventory reporting and simple reorder suggestions.

Key features:

  • Sales analytics and inventory reports
  • Basic reorder point suggestions based on sales velocity
  • Low-stock and out-of-stock alerts
  • Inventory valuation and cost reporting
  • Free plan with core features included

Pricing: Free plan available. Paid plans from approximately $9/month for additional reports and features.

Pros:

  • Free plan covers the basics — good for merchants just starting out
  • Very easy to set up and understand
  • No financial commitment to get value from it

Cons:

  • Not true AI demand forecasting — primarily analytics and basic reorder alerts
  • No variant-level forecasting on the free plan
  • Will not scale effectively beyond a few hundred SKUs
  • Shopify-only

Best for: Merchants with under 100 SKUs who want to move beyond pure guesswork without spending money yet. A strong stepping stone before upgrading to a full forecasting tool.

Rating: 3/5

5. Excel + Demand Forecasting Templates

Excel is not a demand forecasting tool — but it is what the majority of ecommerce merchants start with, and a well-structured spreadsheet genuinely works up to around 50–100 SKUs. The key is using the right formulas and structure from the start.

What you need to make Excel work effectively:

  • A rolling 12-month sales history by SKU
  • A seasonal adjustment factor by month (compare this year vs last year)
  • Supplier lead times for each product
  • A safety stock formula that accounts for demand variability

We offer a free demand forecasting Excel template that includes all of the above in a ready-to-use format — it is the most downloaded resource on this site.

Pricing: Free (assuming you already have Microsoft 365 or use Google Sheets)

Pros:

  • Free and available to everyone
  • Full control over your formulas and forecasting logic
  • No integration, no setup, no subscription
  • Good for understanding how forecasting actually works before buying software

Cons:

  • Breaks down at scale — updating 200+ SKUs manually every week is not sustainable
  • No automatic Shopify or WooCommerce sync — you are manually exporting CSVs
  • Human error risk compounds over time
  • No AI or machine learning — the forecasting model is only as good as your formulas

Best for: Merchants under $500K revenue or with under 50 SKUs who want to understand forecasting before investing in a tool. Download our free Excel template to get started today.

Rating: 3/5 (for the right stage of business)

More Demand Forecasting Tools Worth Considering

Inventory Planner — Best for Multi-Channel Merchants

Inventory Planner (now part of Brightpearl) is a well-established forecasting tool with integrations across Shopify, WooCommerce, Amazon, Etsy, and other channels. It is a strong choice if you sell across three or more platforms and need a single consolidated forecast view. Starting from approximately $99/month. Requires more setup time than the Shopify-native tools but handles multi-channel complexity well.

Brightpearl — Best for Omnichannel Retail Operations

Brightpearl is a full retail operations platform that includes demand forecasting, inventory management, order management, warehouse management, and financials in one system. It is significantly more complex and expensive than the tools above — suited to merchants doing $5M+ with warehouse operations and a dedicated operations team. Quote-based pricing. Typically requires a professional implementation engagement before go-live.

Netstock — Best for Mid-Market Distribution

Netstock is an inventory optimisation platform that connects to ERP systems including SAP, Sage, Syspro, and others. It is not built for Shopify merchants — it is designed for distributors and wholesalers who already have ERP infrastructure and need advanced inventory policy management layered on top. Quote-based pricing. If you are a Shopify or WooCommerce merchant, this is not the right tool.

Anaplan — Enterprise Demand Planning

Anaplan is an enterprise-grade connected planning platform used by large businesses for demand planning, sales forecasting, financial planning, and supply chain scenario modelling. It requires professional implementation, a dedicated system administrator, and a budget measured in the tens of thousands of dollars annually. Included here only because it appears in broad "demand forecasting tools" searches — it is not relevant for ecommerce merchants.

Lokad — Best for Probabilistic Forecasting

Lokad is a specialized demand forecasting platform built around probabilistic forecasting — meaning it gives you a range of likely outcomes rather than a single number, which helps with safety stock decisions. It is primarily used by mid-market and enterprise businesses in retail, manufacturing, and distribution. More technically complex than the ecommerce-focused tools on this list. Quote-based pricing. Not recommended for Shopify merchants without a data analyst on the team.

Blue Yonder (formerly JDA) — Enterprise Supply Chain

Blue Yonder is an enterprise supply chain platform used by large retailers and manufacturers for end-to-end demand planning, inventory optimization, and fulfillment. It is a multi-million dollar implementation that typically takes 12–18 months to deploy. Only relevant here because it appears frequently in "best demand forecasting software" searches — it is not a tool for ecommerce merchants.

Streamline — Best for Distributors and Wholesalers

Streamline is a demand planning and inventory optimization tool positioned between SMB apps and enterprise platforms. It connects to ERP systems like QuickBooks, SAP, and Microsoft Dynamics and is designed for distributors and wholesalers managing hundreds to thousands of SKUs across multiple warehouses. Starting from approximately $149/month. Good option if you have outgrown Excel and are selling wholesale or B2B, but not the right fit for a standard Shopify storefront.

5 Signs You Have Outgrown Your Current Demand Forecasting Approach

Not sure whether you actually need a forecasting tool? These are the most common signs that your current approach — whether that is Excel, gut feel, or no system at all — is costing you money:

1. You are regularly stocking out of your best sellers

If you frequently discover that your top products are out of stock when customers want them, you are losing sales you cannot recover. Stockouts cost the average ecommerce business 4–8% of annual revenue. A demand forecasting tool calculates how much you need to reorder and when — before you run out.

2. You have dead stock sitting in your warehouse

The flip side of stockouts is overstock: slow-moving inventory that ties up cash and takes up space. If you regularly end seasons or quarters with significant unsold stock, your forecasting is too optimistic. A good tool flags overstocked SKUs early so you can discount proactively rather than reactively.

3. Your inventory spreadsheet takes more than two hours a week to maintain

When you have 50–100 SKUs, a spreadsheet is manageable. When you reach 200+ SKUs, updating it weekly becomes a part-time job. If you are spending two or more hours per week copying data from Shopify into Excel and recalculating formulas, a forecasting tool will recoup that time immediately.

4. You are buying inventory based on instinct rather than data

If your purchase orders are driven by "what feels right" or "what sold well last time," you are missing the systematic analysis of trends, seasonality, lead times, and variability that separates good inventory management from guesswork. Even a basic tool like our free Excel template is a step up from pure instinct.

5. You cannot answer "how much stock do I need for the next 60 days?" in under five minutes

This is the simplest test. If answering that question requires you to pull multiple reports, open several spreadsheet tabs, and do manual calculations, your process does not scale. A demand forecasting tool should answer that question automatically, for every SKU, updated daily.

How to Switch From Excel to a Demand Forecasting Tool

The most common concern merchants raise about switching from spreadsheets to software is data migration. In practice, the transition is simpler than most people expect:

  1. Connect your store: Tools like Verve AI pull your sales history directly from Shopify or WooCommerce via API — no CSV upload required
  2. Set lead times: Enter your average supplier lead times per product (most tools have a simple form for this)
  3. Review the first forecast: The tool will generate its first forecasts within minutes using your historical data
  4. Tune over time: Flag any obvious anomalies (a one-off promotional spike that inflated a forecast) and the model adjusts

Most merchants are generating useful purchase order recommendations within a day of setup. The learning curve is not the tool — it is trusting the data over your instincts in the first few weeks.

Which Demand Forecasting Tool Is Right for You?

Best for Shopify merchants

Verve AI or Prediko. Choose Verve AI if you want AI-powered forecasting at the lowest price point with the fastest setup. Choose Prediko if you want tighter purchase order management embedded inside the Shopify admin and you do not sell on WooCommerce.

Best for WooCommerce merchants

Verve AI. It is the only dedicated AI forecasting tool on this list with native WooCommerce support. Most competitors in this space are Shopify-only.

Best free option

Assisty for a basic app, or our free Excel template if you want more control over your forecast logic. Neither will scale beyond a few hundred SKUs, but both work well for getting started without spending money.

Best for replacing Excel

Verve AI. The most common transition we see is merchants moving from a manually-maintained spreadsheet to Verve AI. The time savings typically justify the $24.99/month cost within the first week.

Best for high-volume Shopify stores (500+ orders/month)

Forecastly or Verve AI. Forecastly has stronger built-in analytics for very large catalogues; Verve AI handles variant-level complexity more effectively and has a faster setup.

Best for multi-channel (Shopify + Amazon + wholesale)

Inventory Planner is the most mature option for merchants with three or more active sales channels. More setup effort required but the consolidated view across channels is genuinely useful at that scale.

Frequently Asked Questions About Demand Forecasting Tools

What is the best demand forecasting software for small businesses?

For small ecommerce businesses on Shopify or WooCommerce, Verve AI is the best combination of price, accuracy, and ease of setup. It starts at $24.99/month and requires no developer to get running. For merchants with under 50 SKUs who are not yet ready to pay for software, our free demand forecasting Excel template is a solid starting point.

What are demand planning tools?

Demand planning tools are software platforms that help businesses predict future customer demand and use those predictions to make better decisions about inventory, purchasing, production, and promotions. At the ecommerce level, the practical output is usually a recommended purchase order: "order X units of SKU Y by date Z." Demand planning tools and demand forecasting tools are often used interchangeably, though demand planning technically refers to the broader process of acting on a forecast.

How much does demand forecasting software cost?

For Shopify and WooCommerce merchants, expect to pay $25–$100/month for a capable AI forecasting tool. Enterprise platforms start at several thousand dollars per month and require implementation projects. The tools reviewed here range from free (Assisty, Excel) to $24.99/month (Verve AI) to $99/month (Inventory Planner) to quote-based (Brightpearl, Netstock, Anaplan).

Can I use demand forecasting without a developer?

Yes — all of the Shopify App Store tools on this list (Verve AI, Prediko, Forecastly, Assisty) are no-code and can be installed and configured without developer involvement. Enterprise platforms like Anaplan and Netstock typically require professional implementation consultants and data integration work.

What is the difference between demand forecasting and inventory management?

Demand forecasting predicts how much of each product you will sell in a future period. Inventory management is the broader ongoing process of tracking, ordering, receiving, and controlling your stock. Good demand forecasting feeds directly into inventory management decisions — you cannot manage inventory well without a reasonably accurate forecast of what you will need.

Which demand forecasting tools integrate with Shopify?

Verve AI, Prediko, Forecastly, Assisty, and Inventory Planner all have native Shopify App Store integrations. Brightpearl also integrates with Shopify but requires a more complex setup process. Enterprise tools like Anaplan and Netstock do not have direct Shopify integrations.

The Bottom Line

If you run a Shopify or WooCommerce store and you are still managing inventory with spreadsheets, the tools in this guide will save you significant time and reduce both stockouts and overstock. The question is not whether to use forecasting software — it is which one fits your current stage of growth.

Our recommendation for most merchants: start with Verve AI. It is purpose-built for ecommerce, works on both Shopify and WooCommerce, starts at $24.99/month, and takes under 30 minutes to set up. If you are not ready to invest yet, download our free demand forecasting Excel template and use it until you outgrow it — then switch.

If you are Shopify-only and want tighter purchase order management embedded inside your admin, evaluate Prediko alongside Verve AI before deciding. For multi-channel operations across three or more platforms, Inventory Planner is the most mature option.
